user_message: Help me prep the competitive positioning for Litware Inc. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 09:53 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Litware Inc

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 08:33 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Litware Inc

There's been a change in our timeline for competitive positioning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 10:03 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Litware Inc competitive positioning

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-03-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is competitive positioning.

[00:05:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:08:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:18:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:27:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?
